message:
fix(codec): preserve io::Error kind in LinesCodecError and add cooperative write budget to poll_flush

- LinesCodecError gains an Io(io::Error) variant so that I/O errors from
  the transport layer are no longer silently mapped to InvalidUtf8. The
  new variant exposes source() forwarding and an io_kind() accessor.
  Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq are dropped since io::Error is not equatable.
- poll_flush in FramedWrite now enforces MAX_WRITE_PASSES_PER_POLL = 32,
  mirroring the existing read-side cooperative budget, and self-reschedules
  via wake_by_ref() on overflow to avoid starving other tasks on a
  slow-draining transport.
- codec_e2e tests updated to use matches!() instead of == comparisons
  now that LinesCodecError is no longer PartialEq.
- New framed_read test confirms io::Error kind survives the
  LinesCodec → FramedRead → caller path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
